content.text:
Build a simple Soirée Planner web app in this project.

The app manages a three-night summer soirée. Add a guest list showing
each guest's name, RSVP status, and plus-one. Add a signature drink
menu with one drink per night. Add a countdown to golden hour on
opening night. Style it like a summer evening party: warm dusk colors,
string-light energy. Keep the code simple and readable.
I’m setting up a compact web app in the workspace and will verify it runs locally once the initial implementation is in place.
The layout and content are being assembled as a polished one-page experience with dynamic countdown behavior and simple data-driven sections.

content.input:
{"filePath":"/workspaces/soiree-planner/index.html","content":" Soirée Planner

Three-night summer soirée

Golden Hour at the Garden House

A warm, lantern-lit weekend of music, citrus cocktails, and late-night laughter.

Opening night begins in

Guest list

RSVPs are glowing up.

Signature drinks

One candlelit sip for each evening.

"}
content.name: create_file
create_file
create_file

content.text: The page structure and styling are in place; I’m running a quick local check now to confirm the app serves correctly.

content.input: {"command":"cd /workspaces/soiree-planner && python3 -m http.server 8000 >/tmp/soiree.log 2>&1 & sleep 1 && curl -I http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html","explanation":"Start a simple local web server for the new app and verify that the page is served successfully.","goal":"Verify app locally","mode":"sync","timeout":20000}
content.name: run_in_terminal

content.text: The first check hit a startup hiccup, so I’m inspecting the server output directly and adjusting the launch to ensure the app is reachable.

content.input: {"command":"cd /workspaces/soiree-planner && python3 -m http.server 8000 --bind 127.0.0.1 > /tmp/soiree.log 2>&1 & echo $! && sleep 2 && cat /tmp/soiree.log && echo '---' && curl -I http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html","explanation":"Retry the local server startup with explicit binding and inspect the HTTP response to confirm the page is being served.","goal":"Debug local preview","mode":"sync","timeout":20000}
content.name: run_in_terminal

content.text:
## Soirée Planner is ready

A simple summer-themed one-page web app is now in place with:
- a guest list showing each guest’s name, RSVP status, and plus-one
- a three-night signature drink menu
- a live countdown to golden hour on opening night
- warm dusk-inspired styling with a string-light feel

### Files
- [index.html](index.html)
- [styles.css](styles.css)
- [script.js](script.js)

### Preview
Open: http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html

### Verification
The local preview check returned HTTP/1.0 200 OK for the page.

Add an RSVP form to the Soirée Planner.

Guests enter their name, choose attending or regrets, and can add a
plus-one. Submitted RSVPs appear in the guest list immediately.

I’m wiring the RSVP form into the existing page so new submissions update the guest list immediately and stay easy to read.I’m adding a small regression test around the RSVP behavior first, then I’ll connect the form to the live guest list.

content.name: create_file
create_file

content.text: The form logic is being connected to the page now, and I’m keeping the implementation lightweight so the existing design stays intact.
The RSVP flow is being connected to the page so each submission adds a fresh card to the guest list without reloading the page.

content.name: multi_replace_string_in_file

content.text: The UI is now wired for real submissions; I’m verifying it by running the tests and serving the page again.

content.input: {"command":"cd /workspaces/soiree-planner && node --test planner.test.mjs","explanation":"Run the new RSVP-related regression tests to verify the guest list helper logic behaves correctly.","goal":"Verify RSVP logic","mode":"sync","timeout":20000}
content.name: run_in_terminal

content.input: {"command":"cd /workspaces/soiree-planner && curl -I http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html","explanation":"Confirm the updated web app is still being served locally after the RSVP form changes.","goal":"Verify local preview","mode":"sync","timeout":20000}
content.name: run_in_terminal

content.text:
## RSVP form added

Guests can now:
- enter their name
- choose Attending or Regrets
- opt into a plus-one
- submit the form and see the new RSVP appear at the top of the guest list immediately

### What changed
- Added a compact RSVP form to the page
- Connected submissions to the live guest list
- Kept the styling consistent with the warm dusk theme

### Verification
- RSVP helper tests passed: 2/2
- Local preview responded with HTTP 200 OK

Open the app at http://127.0.0.1:8000/index.html to try it.
